more: n. m. A large sandy tract of country; a sandy desert. reg-mālī , n. f. Polishing with sandpaper.
(ريگ Sand, and ستان or استان Place) s. m. A sandy place, a strand.
subs: s.f. Moving or shifting sand, quicksand; sand agitated as waves (by the wind)
reg-istān, s.m. Sandy region or tract, sandy desert, sandy place
s.f. Rubbing or polishing with sand or sand-paper
s.f. lit. 'Sand-fish'; the skink, Lacerta scincus (used, in a dried state, in medicine).
ex: ریگستان میں بہت كم چیزیں اُگ سکتی ہیں۔
registān mẽ bahot kam cīzẽ ug saktī h͠ai.
Very few things can grow in the desert.
صحرائے اعظم دنیا کا سب سے بڑا ریگستان ہے۔
sahrā-e a'zam dunyā kā sab se baṛā registān hai.
The Sahara is the largest desert in the world.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ریگِسْتَان (rēgistān). By surface analysis, ریگ (reg) + ـِستان (-istān).

def: n. f. A lizard, used in medicine in a dried state; skink; Lacerta scincus .
s. m. A sandy region or place.

def: s.f. Fossil alkali (used for washing and making soap), impure nitrate of soda, fuller's earth (it abounds in some soils as an efflorescence, rendering them altogether unproductive); — brackish or barren soil.
more: n. f. An alkali; impure nitrate of soda; fuller's earth. It abounds in some soils as an efflorescence, rendering them unproductive even for grass.

def: s. f. ( miṭṭī ) Fossil alkali, used for washing and making soap.
adj. Impregnated with alkali (said of soil), brackish, barren.
adj. Impregnated with alkali (said of soil), brackish, barren.
adj. Reh land; barren saline soil.

s.m. = rahlā, q.v. s.m. The chick-pea, 'gram,' Cicer arietinum (syn. ćanā); a vetch.
s. m. A vetch.

ety: for A. رحلة, inf. n. of un. fr. رحل; see raḥl
def: s.f. Removal, departure, journey; departure from this life, death, decease
more: n. f. رحل saddling a camel. 1. Marching. 2. Departure to the other world; death.
s. f. Marching, departure (particularly to the other world); death, decease. رحلت کرنا riḥlat karnā, v. n. To depart, to die.
subs: v.n. To take (one's) departure; to depart this life, to die.

def: v.t. To mark with lines, to streak, to delineate.

ety: reh, q.v.+ṛī = aṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. अ+र+इका
def: s.f. Sandy or barren soil, brackisk land or soil.
n. f. Sandy or saline soil.
s. m. A sled, a sledge.
(i.q. rahṛū, q.v.), A cart, sled, sledge. s.m. A cart (cf. rehṛū); a light, open cart (with one seat); a go-cart (syn. gaḍūlnā).

s.f. = rahū, rohū, q.v. s.m. (Myth.) Name of a Daitya or demon (son of Vipra-ćitti and Sinhikā) who is supposed to seize the sun and moon and thus cause eclipses; (in Astron.) the dragon's head, the ascending node of a planet; — rāhu-ketu, vulg. rāhū-get, s.m. The ascending and descending nodes of a planet
subs: s.m. Eclipse of the sun or moon.

def: n. S. रोहित A kind of fish.
s.f. Abundance, &c. ( = rel-pel, q.v.).
s. f. Abundance.
s.f. = rekhān, q.v. s.f. Land that is beyond the reach of river water.

def: s.f. = ريکهہ rekh, q.v.
s. f. Writing, line, mark, fate, destiny.
s.f. A line, &c. ( = rekhā, q.v.); a black line (made with missī) on the teeth
subs: The whiskers, &c. to begin to appear, to have down on the cheek
or rekh-rūp, s.m. Lineaments and complexion, features and countenance, shape and form, the contour and the tout-ensemble.

def: s.f. Line; streak, stripe; row, series; mark; writing; a furrow; lines on the palm of the hand; fate, destiny; (in Astron.), the first meridian, the line drawn from Lankā (Ceylon) to Meru, i.e. from the equator to the north pole; fulness, satisfaction; — deceit, fraud; — a small quantity, a little
more: n. f. 1. A line ( ḍaṅḍīr ). 2. The lines on the palm of the hand, believed to have been inscribed by God. rekhā prati rekhā , n. f. Interlineation. rekh bhīgnā , v. n. To have down on the cheeks. Tumhre barābar nāhīṅ, haiṅ, bhaiyā! tum lauhure, ham jeṭh , Hamre āivā baṛī baṛī muchhvā, tumre bhījat rekh . Song. Birhā. I'm not your equal, you are young, and I'm your elder known, I have a big moustache, while, brother, yours is only down. rekhā-ganit , n. f. Geometry. karam-rekh, yā rekhā n. m. Fate; destiny. 1. Karam rekh nā miṭe, karo koī lākhan chaturāī . Prov. The lines of fate you cant destroy though hundred thousand arts you try. 2. Tulsī! rekhā karam kī meṭ na sakke Rām, Meṭeṅ to achraj nahīṅ, samajh kīyā hai kām . Tulsī Tulsī! the lines of Fate e'en Rām cannot destroy, He could, but wisdom great he did afore employ.
s. f. Writing, line, mark, fate, destiny.
wety: From Sanskrit रेखा (rekhā).

def: s.f. Land that is beyond the reach of river water.
s.m. See s.v. rekh.

ety: perf. part. of reḵẖtan, rt. Zend rić = S. रिच्; cf. S. रेचित
def: part. adj. Poured out; scattered; mixed; — s.m. 'The mixed dialect,' the Hindūstānī or Urdū language (as used by men, cf. reḵẖtī); — a Hindūstānī ode; — mortar, plaster.
s.f. Hindūstānī verse written in the language of women, and expressing the sentiments, &c. peculiar to them. (The two principal writers in this idiom are the poets Rangīn and Jān Sāḥib.)
more: n. f. Hindustani verse written in the tones and idioms of women with their peculiar sentiments and characteristics. Rangīn was the first Rekhtī poet. He has been followed by Jān Sāhab of Lucknow.

def: s.m. Rail, railway
more: n. f. A Railway train or carriage. Jab se chalī rel-gāṛī kadar shikram kī bigāṛī, Kunjṛe bhī roveṅ, banye bhī roveṅ, saṛkoṅ pe roveṅ bhaṭyārī . Song of the Railway. Since Railway trains began to run dāk-gāris have gone out, Green grocers weep, and Banyas weep, and inn keepers throughout. rel kī saṛak , n. f. A Railway. mahakma-i-rel , Railway department.

def: s. (dakh.) Abundance, in abundance.
s.f. Crowd; abundance, &c. (see rel-pel, the more com. form)
subs: s.f. A continuous line (of people, &c.), crowd, throng, press, crush (cf. rewaṛ); abundance, profusion, exuberance, glut (in a market)
To crowd, throng; to press, push, jostle; to supply in abundance, to overstock, to glut (a market)

def: s.m. A line or string of animals; a rushing stream, a torrent, a flood, inundation; — rushing, pushing, rush, push, press, crush, shove, assault
more: n. m. 1. A torrent; tide; flood; inundation. 2. The jostling and hustling of a crowd.
s. m. 1. A flood. 2. A line or string of animals. 3. Rushing, rush, push, shove, assault.
subs: (-ko), To give a push, to push, &c. ( = relnā, q.v.).

ety: prob. fr. S. प्रेर्; cf. pelnā
def: v.t. To push, shove, jostle, hustle.
more: v. a. 1. To shove; push. 2. To jostle; hustle.
v. a. To shove, to push, to rush.

def: n. f. 1. ( rahalpahal ) A glut in the market; plenty ( bahutāt ). Dekhā, Mīrdād, terā rambā! Gājroṅ kī rahal-pahal, roṭiyoṅ kā chambā . Prov. I have seen, Mīr Dad, your kingdom rare, Of carrots plenty, but of bread a scare! 2. A crowd ( bhīṛ ). rel-pel k. , v. a. 1. To overstock; glut the market. 2. To supply in abundance. 3. To push about; hustle.
more: s. f. 1. Abundance, plenty. 2. Crowd, bustle.

def: s.m. = رينو reṇu, q.v. s.m.f. Dust; sand; powder; a grain of dust or sand, &c.; an atom; grit; pollen (of flowers).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi رین (ryn) (c. 1611), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀭𑀬𑀡𑀻 (rayaṇī), from Sanskrit रजनी (rajanī́), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hráȷ́aniH, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *HráǰaniH, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁régʷenih₂, from *h₁régʷos (“darkness”).

def: 1. Crying or moaning (of a child). 2. The discordant note or scraping of a stringed instrument.
s.f. The Palma christi or castor-oil plant (the more common form is renṛ; — see reṅḍī).
s.f. The Palma christi, Ricinus vulgaris ( = reṅḍ or reṅṛ); — the berry or seed of the Palma christi
more: s. f. Palma christi (Ricinus vulgaris). رينڐي کا تيل renḍī kā tel, s. m. Castor-oil.

ety: prob. reṅd, dialec. for retī, and reṅṭ, qq.v.+S. aff. इका or ईया
def: s.f. A small melon (syn. ḵẖarbuza).
more: n. f. A small melon. kachchī reṅdī , n. f. ( Slang ) A raw and inexperienced person; a little child.

def: s.m. corr. of رينو reṇu, q.v. s.m.f. Dust; sand; powder; a grain of dust or sand, &c.; an atom; grit; pollen (of flowers).
s.f. Creeping, crawling.
v.t. To cause to creep or crawl, &c.
s.f. Sciatica, hipgout; spasm (of a limb); — the plant Solanum jacquini (syn. bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā).
more: E. n. f. See bhaṭ kaṭaiyā . A medicinal herb; solanum jacquinī , Wat.
s. f. Name of a medicinal herb. See بهٿ کٿيا bhaṭ kaṭaiyā.

ety: reṅk(nā)+ṭ = Prk. aḍa = S. ṭ+ā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A young ass, foal of an ass.

n. m. The young of an ass. Kaheṅ Girdhar kabirāë, gadhaiyoṅ ke bhī reṅṭā, Samaë palat ān bāp se jhagṛat beṭā . Girdhar kabirāë. The poet Girdhar says, 'an ass's colt e'en you shall see, Full-grown, his turn now come, fights with his old sire constantly. reṅg'nā, reṅknā ; Tir. fut. rekab . v. a. To bray as an ass.
s.f. Braying (of an ass).
more: (from رينکنا) s. f. Braying (as, of an ass).

ety: reṅk˚ = Prk. रेक्क(इ), or रेक्के(इ), fr. S. रेष् (acc. sing. neut. रेट्)+कृ

more: v. n. S. रै to sound. 1. To bellow as a buffalo. 2. To bray as an ass.
v. n. To bray (as, an ass).

s.f. Constant crying or whimpering (of a child); scraping or discordant sounds (of a stringed instrument).

s.m. corr. of rahaṭ, q.v. s.f. Quiet, rest, repose, ease, tranquillity, cessation of toil or trouble or inconvenience, freedom from toil or trouble, &c., relief; pleasure
more: s. m. 1. The same as رهٿ rahaṭ, q. v. 2. Snivel, snot.
subs: adj. Quieting, soothing, promoting or adding to (one's) comfort, &c.
vulg. rāḥat-jān, s.f. lit. 'Comfort of the soul'; green chillies chopped up and mixed with salt and lime-juice.

n. m. The fruit of the lhesvā tree.

ety: Pehl. resh; Zend raêsha, rt. rish; S. रेष, rt. रिष्, (रेषति)
def: s.m. Wound; sore; scar; pus, matter; — (in comp.) wounding, wounded (e.g. dil-resh, 'wounding, or wounded in, the heart').
more: s. m. A wound, a sore, pus, matter. In compos. adj. Wounded; as جگرريش jigar-resh, or دل ريش dil-resh, Wounded to the heart or in the heart.
wety: Derived from Classical Persian ریش (rīš, “beard”).

ety: prob. Zend raêsha; cf. rīsh; and resmān
def: s.m. Fibre; filament; nerve; vein (of a leaf); stringiness (of a mango, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Fibre; filament; nerve. 2. ( Bot .) A vein. 3. Stringiness (of a mango). reshe-dār , adj. ( Phys .) Fibrous; stringy.
s. m. Fibre, stringiness of a mango. ريشه دار resha-dār, adj. Fibrous, stringy.
wety: PIE word *wréh₂ds Borrowed from Classical Persian ریشه (rēša, “root; stump”). Compare Baluchi ریشگ (rīšag, “root”)

def: s.m. Silk (thread, or cloth)
more: n. m. Silk. 1. kujā resham, kujā pasham? Prov. Wool with silk! resham, resham ke lachchhe , adj. lit . a skein of silk. Soft and smooth as silk. resh' am-vālā , n. m. A dealer in silk or silk thread.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ریشم (rēšam), a variant of ابریشم (abrēšam, abrēšum, “silk”), from Middle Persian ʾp̄lyš(w)m (abrēšom, “silk”).

ety: resham, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Silken, made or woven of silk.
more: adj. 1. Silken; woven of silk. Ṭāṭ kī aṅgyā reshmī tanī! Prov. A canvass jacket, and cords of silk!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ریشمی (rēšamī), a variant of ابریشمی (abrēšamī). By surface analysis, ریشم (reśam) + ـی (-ī).

ety: resham, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Silken, made or woven of silk.

ety: res or rīs, fr. ristan, 'to spin,' &c., rt. Zend rīć = S. रिच्+mān = Zend man = S. मन्; cf. S. रश्मन्
def: s.f. String, cord, thread, rope ( = rassī, q.v.). s.f. Rope, line, string, cord, reins; a measuring line (about 100 or 120 cubits long); — a snake (used superstitiously, especially by women, who believe that uttering the word 'snake' will cause one to appear).
more: s. f. String, cord, thread, rope. (Sans. रश्मि ).

def: adj. = reshmī, q.v. adj. Silken, made or woven of silk.
s.f. (dialec.), Envy; emulation ( = rīs, q.v.). s.f. Anger, passion, vexation, displeasure (conceived), offence, huff
s.f. Sand; filings.
more: n. m. S. रेणु 1. Sand. 2. Filing.
s. m. Sperma genitale (viri aut mulieris), seed.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀭𑁂𑀢𑁆𑀢 (*retta), from Sanskrit रेत्र (retra), perhaps of non-Aryan origin; see there for more.

def: s. m. (dakh.) Snot: see رينٿ renṭ, sign. 2.
s.m. Sand; filings ( = ret).
s.m. = retas, q.v. s.m. Seminal fluid, semen, sperm, sperma genitale; seed, offspring; quicksilver (regarded as Śiva's semen).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀭𑁂𑀢𑁆𑀢 (*retta), from Sanskrit रेत्र (retra), perhaps of non-Aryan origin; see there for more.

def: s.m. = retas, q.v. s.m. Seminal fluid, semen, sperm, sperma genitale; seed, offspring; quicksilver (regarded as Śiva's semen).
s.f. Price paid for filing, &c. (see retnā).
n. f. The price paid for filing.
s. f. Price paid for filing or polishing.

more: adj. Sandy. s. f. Sand, sandy ground.

s.m. Seminal fluid, semen, sperm, sperma genitale; seed, offspring; quicksilver (regarded as Śiva's semen).
s.f. Sandy ground on the bank of a river, a tract of alluvial land.
more: n. f. 1. Sandy soil (4th quality). 2. A tract of alluvial land. Daryā kī rctī ke tāzoṅ hī kā mazā ! ( Bhuṭṭā maize) Delhi Street cry. Fresh Indian corn from the river sands! See.خالي 1- 4.
s. f. 1. Sandy ground on the shore of a river. 2. A file.

def: s.f. A file ( = retnī).
adj. (f. -ī), Sandy; gravelly; gritty ( = retal).

def: v.t. To file, rasp; to polish.
v. a. To file, to polish.

ety: for retilā = ret+Prk. अइल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = retal, q.v. adj. Abounding in sand, sandy, gravelly; gritty; — s.f. Sandy ground or soil, sand ( = retlī, q.v.).
s.f. Sandy ground or soil; a tract of alluvial land ( = retī).
v.t. To file, rasp; to polish; to thrum.
more: v. a. H. ret , filings. To file; rasp.
v. a. To file, to polish, to thrum.

def: s.f. A file ( = retī).
s.m. A filer; a polisher.
more: s. m. A file-man, a filer, polisher.

def: s.f. Trick, fraud, deceit, guile.

ety: prob. S. रेणु; or S. रेवा, rt. रेव्
def: s.f. (dialec.) Sand, fine gravel; grit; grain (of sand, sugar, &c.); a shoal, a ford (cf. rewṛī).
s.f. Name of the Narmadā river (which rises in one of the Vindhya mountains called Amarkanṭak, in the province of Gondwānā); — name of Rati, wife of Kām-deva.
corr. of rewand, q.v.

def: s. f. Rhubarb. See راوند.
s.f. The flower Jasminum angustifolium.

ety: prob. S. रेव (rt. रेव्)+ṛ = Prk. डी = S. र (or ट)+ई; — cf. rel, relā

wety: From Sanskrit रेवण (revaṇa).

def: n. m. A flock of sheep or goats.
s.m. = rebās, q.v. s.m. Sorrel; the plant of which rhubarb is the root, Rheum ribes, or R. palmatum (cf. rāwand; rewand).
s.m. Name of an ancient king, father of Revatī, and father-in-law of Balarām.
more: s. m. Name of a Rājā, who was father in law of Balarāma.

def: s.f. Name of a daughter of Revat and wife of Balarām; name of the twenty-seventh (or the fifth) Nakshatra or lunar asterism (consisting of Piscium ζ and thirty-one other stars, and figured by a tabor); — name of one of the female energies (mātr̤ī) of the gods
more: n. f. The 27th lunar mansion consisting of Piscium and thirty one other stars.
s. f. 1. The twenty-seventh mansion of the moon, consisting of ζ Piscium and thirty one other stars. 2. n. prop. The name of Balarāmaʼs wife.

ety: S. रेव+ṛī = Prk. डिआ = र (or ट)+इका
def: s.f. A kind of sweetmeat (a small cake of solidified sugar covered with sesamum-seeds)
more: s. f. A kind of sweetmeat. ريوڙي کي پهيرمين پڙنا rewṛī ke pher meṅ paṛnā, v. n. To be involved in difficulties. (This alludes to a game founded on the principle of geometrical progression, wherein one party bets that he will eat one of these sweet cakes so many times doubled, and is confounded at finding it amount to a quantity far beyond his expectation).
subs: 'To fall into the complication of the reʼoṛī'; to be involved in difficulties, to find oneself in a difficulty, to be disconcerted or discomfited (the allusion is to a game founded on the principle of geometrical progression, where one bets that he will eat the sum of the reoṛīs obtained by doubling one reoṛī a certain number of times, and is confounded at finding it amount to a quantity far exceeding his expectation).

def: n. f. A sweetmeat. revṛī ke pher meṅ ānā yā paṛnā , v. n. lit . to be caught by sweets; hence, to be drawn into or involved in a difficulty, etc. The phrase is derived from a bet which one boy lays against another that he cant eat a certain quantity of revṛīs and at the same time shake one of his fingers without stopping, the attempt being usually unsuccessful. Others derive it from a problem in geometrical progression, as 'I bet you, you dont eat one reoṛī , doubling it each time'. Abhī revṛī ke pher meṅ nahīṅ āë ho .

ety: fr. reḵẖtan, imperat. rez; rt. Zend rić = S. रिच्
def: part. Pouring, scattering, dropping, shedding, infusing, applying, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ashk-rez, 'shedding tears'; dur-rez, 'scattering pearls'; rang-rez, 'a painter'); — s.f. A small thing, anything crumbled or reduced to powder; a grain, scrap, bit, a crumb ( = reza), a drop, draught; — a favour, mercy; — profusion, abundance, affluence, wealth; — a cup, goblet, bowl; — will, desire, wish, concupiscence
more: to shed. ( In comp .) Pouring; scattering; dropping; shedding, as khūṅrez a murderer, arq-rezī hard labour. rez , n. f. Abundance ( ifrāt ). rez k. , v. n. To chirp or speak (young birds, Contemp . men.) Dekho, kyā maze se totā, totī rez kar rahe haiṅ .

part. act. (of ريختن) in compos. Pouring, scattering, dropping, &c. as, اشک ريز ashk-rez, Shedding tears. درريز durr-rez, Scattering pearls. ريزکرنا rez-karnā, To begin to speak or talk (as, young birds). ¶ s. A draught or drop, a cup, a favour, mercy, desire, wish, a crumb, scrap, bit.
subs: v.t. To pour out or forth, to scatter, to shed; to begin to chirp or talk (as young birds).

r: reza, rez, lit, rezgī, thān, reza-ćīn
def: adj. & s.m. Picking up scraps; — one who picks up scraps
more: n. m. 1. An atom; particle; filing. 2. (Rus. rejā ) A piece of cloth ( thān ). Rāṅgī chādar mūl na oḍhūṅ, dhaulī āj sivāūṅgī, Sagrā rejā dhobī dhobyā darjī ke le jāūṅgī . Rus. song. A mantle white I will get made, I will not colors wear, With the piece of cloth by laundress washed to the tailor I'II repaid. 3. ( Slang ) A beautiful young damsel; a young girl adopted by a prostitute. 4. A laboring boy (Masons) ( rezgī , 4.). Chālīs baras kā rezah . Prov. A forty years' child. (An old child). 5. ( Mech .) Moulding box. rezah rezah k. , v. a. To break into pieces; to shiver; shatter. rezah rezah h. , v. n. To be broken in small fragments, smashed, etc.

s. m. 1. A scrap, piece, bit. 2. Small coin (as, four-rupee-pieces, &c.). 3. A kind of cock. 4. Children employed in mason-work, &c. who receive half, one third, &c. of a manʼs pay. ريزه چين reza-chīn, adj. Picker up of scraps. ريزه چيني reza-chīn-ī, s. f. Picking up of scraps. ريزه خط reza-ḵẖat̤t̤, Fine striped, thin-lined. ريزه ريزه reza reza, 1. s. m. Scraps, pieces. 2. adj. In pieces, broken in pieces, small.
subs: s.f. The picking up of scraps
adj. & s.m. Eating scraps or remnants (of food); — one who eats scraps, &c.
adj. & adv. In bits, in pieces, broken in pieces, small; — s.m. Scraps, pieces
v.t. To break in pieces, to smash, crush, shatter, shiver
v.n. To be broken in pieces, to be smashed, to be pounded small.

r: rezagī, rezgī, rezak, reza, ī, i
ety: rezak = reza+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. A scrap, bit, piece ( = reza); small coin (as two-anna, four-anna, or eight-anna pieces), change; a small piece of gold or silver wire; a child employed in mason-work ( = reza, q.v.).
more: n. f. 1. A scarp; bit; piece. 2. Small change; a two-anna, four-anna, or eight-anna bit. 3. Small pieces of gold or silver wire. 4. ( rezā ) Children employed in masonry work, etc., who receive half, one-third, etc., of a man's pay.
(from ريزه) s. f. 1. A scrap, a bit, piece. 2. Small coin (as, four-rupee-pieces, &c.), change (small money).

ety: imperf. part. of reḵẖtan; see rez
def: part. Pouring, scattering, shedding, dropping (used as last member of compounds).
more: part. pres. (from ريختن) in compos. Pouring, scattering.

def: s.f. = rezgī, q.v.

ety: rez, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Pouring, scattering, &c. (see rez; — used in comp., e.g. ḵẖūn-rezī, 'blood-shedding).
more: in compos. s. f. Pouring, scattering.

ety: rez, q.v.+ish = Zend shi or she
def: s.f. Pouring out, scattering; flowing in small quantities, running; a running at the nose.
more: n. f. A running at the nose.
(from ريختن) s. f. 1. A running at the nose. 2. A running, flowing in small quantities, pouring out, scattering, effusion.

ety: for A. رعاية, inf. n. of رعي 'to pasture, to tend'
def: s.f. Guarding, protecting, tending, looking (after), taking care (of, -kī), paying attention (to); observance, respect, regard, attention, honour; kindness, favour, patronage, partiality; indulgence, remission, mitigation, abatement; pity, clemency, leniency; — a form of writing in which the ideas introduced correspond with that suggested by the first word (e.g. the word 'garden' requires that the images in the sentences following should be 'verdure,' 'flowers,' 'birds,' &c.)
more: n. f. 1. Partiality; bias. 2. Respect; regard; favor; kindness. 3. Notice; advertence; attention; heed. 4. Mitigation; remission; abatement. 5. A form of writing in which all the images presented, correspond with that suggested by the first word, as 'garden' requires that the images in the sentences following, should be verdure, flowers, birds, etc. 6. Leniency; clemency. riٴaāyat k. , v. a. 1. To be partial or lenient. 2. To show favor or indulgence. 3. To mitigate; remit; relax.

(from رعي Heb. To lead to pasture, to feed, to protect, to observe) s. f. 1. Observance, respect, regard, attention, honour. 2. Kindness, favour. 3. Guarding, protecting, taking care (of), paying attention. 4. Remission, indulgence, favour. رعايت کرنا riʼāﻌyat karnā, To make observance (of), to take care (of).
subs: (-kī), To make observance (of), be careful, take care (of); to pay regard or heed or attention (to); to show favour or indulgence (to), to be partial (to), be lenient, be indulgent; to remit, to mitigate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رعایت (ri'āyat), from Arabic رِعَايَة (riʕāya).

def: adj. & s.m. Favoured, privileged; granted as a favour or indulgence, privilege (leave); — a favoured or privileged person, a protégé; one to whom remission or abatement has been granted.
more: n. m. A favored or privileged person; one to whom remission or abatement has been granted. riāyātī chhuṭṭī yā rukhsat , Privilege leave.
s. m. Favoured person, a protégé.

def: s.m. Rope, fastening, ligament; a building for the accommodation of travellers and their beasts, an inn, a caravansary; a hospice; a religious house (pl. ribāt̤āt).
s.m. The keeper of a ribāt̤, innkeeper, &c.
more: s. m. The keeper of a ribāt̤.

def: s.m. (f. -in, or -nī) = next, q.v.

s.f. Lustre, splendour; — a hymn; a single verse, stanza, or text; — the Rig-veda (so called because it consists chiefly of hymns to the Hindū deities); a verse or text from the Rig-veda; a mystical verse or text from a Veda, magical invocation.

s.f. Lustre, splendour; — a hymn; a single verse, stanza, or text; — the Rig-veda (so called because it consists chiefly of hymns to the Hindū deities); a verse or text from the Rig-veda; a mystical verse or text from a Veda, magical invocation.
more: n. f. A magical invocation; mystical prayers of the Vedas .
s. f. A magical invocation, a mystical prayer of the Vedas.

def: s.m. corr. of r̤ićīk, q.v.
s.m. = r̤iksha; and rīćh, qq.v.
s.m. corr. of riksheś, q.v.s.v. r̤iksha.
s.m. (f. -in, or -nī), A bear
n. m. S. ऋक्ष Pr. richchho . A bear. Baṛā rīchh ; Sant. buṛhī parkom , Ursa major. Chhoṭā rīchh . Ursa minor.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀭𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀙 (riccha), from Sanskrit ऋक्ष (ṛkṣa), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hŕ̥ćšas, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂ŕ̥tḱos.. Doublet of خرس (xirs)

def: s.m. Name of a saint, the father of Jamadagni; — name of a country.

s.f. A cloak or mantle (worn by Dervishes); a cloak or wrapper (put on when going out); an upper garment of fine linen unsewn or in one piece (or sewn, if made of other cloth) reaching to the middle of the leg.
more: s. f. 1. A cloak or mantle (worn by Dervises). 2. An upper garment of fine linen unsewed (or sewed, if made of other cloth), reaching to the middle of the leg; any kind of cloak put on when going out.

def: adj. & s.m. Increased; prosperous, thriving, rising; — a prosperous person, &c.
s. f. Increase, wealth, prosperity.

s.f. Increase, growth; plenty; affluence, wealth, property; success, prosperity, good fortune; accomplishment, perfection; — name of a medicinal plant; prosperity personified as the wife of Kuver; name of the goddess Pārvatī
more: n. f. Pr. riddho . 1. Increase; growth. 2. Wealth; prosperity. 3. A plant.

ety: prob. pl. of rafhuṉ, inf. n. of رفه; used in Persian as a sing.
def: s.f. An easy or comfortable state of life, enjoyment of life, ease, comfort, affluence; content, repose, quiet, tranquillity, relief.
more: (from رفه To live comfortably and at ease. Heb. To be at rest, to desist) s. f. Content, affluence, enjoyment of life, repose, quiet, tranquillity.

def: The good of the community; the public good.
n. f. افر he found ease. 1. Relief ( ārām , 2, 3). 2. Welfare ( behtarī ).
(inf. n. of رفق 'to be gentle, kind, or courteous,' &c.), s.m.? Gentleness, tenderness, kindness, graciousness, benignity, benevolence, courtesy, civility.
s.m. The persuasion, or creed, of a rāfiẓa or heretic; heresy, schism; — (corr. of A. rafẓ, inf. n. of رفض), abandoning, leaving, deserting.
more: (properly rafẓ or rafaẓ ) s. m. 1. Schism, heresy. 2. Abandoning.

def: s.f. = r̤ik; and r̤ig-veda or r̤ig-bed, q.v.s.v r̤ik.
s.m. (local), contrac. of registān, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. r̤ik.
wety: Perhaps a medieval borrowing directly from Sanskrit ऋग्वेद (ṛgvedá) or a loanword via Classical Persian from the same source. Compare Ottoman Turkish وید (vid, “Vedas”).

ety: inf. n. of روح 'to be windy,' &c.
def: s.f. Wind, air; flatus, flatulence; puff, whiff; odour, exhalation
more: n. f. Wind. rīh kā dard , n. m. Gout ( gaṭhiyā ).
(from روح Heb. ) s. f. 1. Wind. 2. Odour, exhalation.

def: adj. Freed; liberated; set at liberty ( barī , 1, 2). rihā k., rihāī denā , v. a. To discharge; relieve ( barī k .)
n. f. رﮩت to be free. 1. Escape; freedom. 2. Exemption; relief ( chhuṭkārā , 1). rihāī ba-zamānat k . To discharge on bail; to accept bail.
s. f. Liberation, escape, deliverance, relief, acquittal, discharge, freedom, release, salvation.

ety: inf. n. of iii of رهن 'to remain,' &c.
def: s.m. Betting on a horse-race.
s.m. pl. (of rahn or rihn), Pledges; bets, stakes.
more: 1. s. plur. (of رهن) Pledges, bets, stakes. 2. Betting on the race of a horse.

def: adj. Windy; flatulent.

s.m. An invisible being which moves in a circular orbit round the world (syn. yoginī. On the 1st, 9th, 16th, and 24th days of a lunar month, his station is in the east; on the 3rd, 11th, 18th, and 26th, in the south-east; on the 5th, 13th, 20th, and 28th, in the south; on the 4th, 12th, 19th, and 27th, in the south-west; on the 6th, 14th, 21st, and 29th, in the west; on the 7th, 15th, and 22nd in the north-west; on the 2nd, 10th, 17th, and 25th, in the north; and on the 8th, 23rd, and 30th, in the north-east. His influence on each day is especially exerted during nine ghaṛīs, or three hours thirty-six minutes, at the close of that tith or lunar day; and, in that interval, it is unfortunate to begin a journey towards the station of this being, or in such a direction as to place him on the right hand).
more: n. m. Pl. of رجل Men.

s. pl. (of رجل) Men. رجال الغيب rijālu-l-g̠ẖaib (by the Hindūs called योगिनी yoginī ) s. m. An invisible being which moves in a circular orbit round the world: on the 1st, 9th, 16th, and 24th days of a lunar month, his station is in the east: on the 3d, 11th, 18th, and 26th, in the south-east: on the 5th, 13th, 20th, and 28th, in the south: on the 4th, 12th, 19th, and 27th, in the south-west: on the 6th, 14th, 21st, and 29th, in the west: on the 7th, 15th, and 22d, in the north-west: on the 2d, 10th, 17th, and 25th, in the north: and, on the 8th, 23d, and 30th, in the north-east. His influence on each day is especially exerted during nine ghaṛis, or three hours thirty six minutes, at the close of that tith or lunar day; and, in that interval, it is unfortunate to begin a journey towards the station of this being, or in such a direction as to place him on the right hand.

def: adj. corr. of رزاله rizāla, q.v. adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Low, mean, base, bad, vile, contemptible; — the worse or viler (or the worst or vilest), the refuse, or dregs (of anything), a thing of which the good has been picked out; a mean, low, or vile person; an impudent or shameless fellow.
more: (for رزاله q. v.) adj. Mean, vulgar, rabble.

def: n. m. An invisible being that moves in a circular orbit round the world.
n. f. S. रञ्ज् to be pleased. Pleasure; gratification; satisfaction. rījh pachā' nā , v. a. To conceal one's gratification. rījh-pachāū , n. m. One who conceals his inclinations.
more: s. f. 1. Choice, approbation, love, desire, wish. 2. Pleasing, satisfying. ريجهـ پچانا rījh pachānā, v. a. To conceal oneʼs gratification. ريجهـ پچاؤ rījh-pachāʼū, s. m. A person who conceals his inclination.

def: s.f. Pleasing, satisfying; pleasure, satisfaction, gratification; desire, wish, inclination; love, liking; approbation
subs: s.m. One who conceals his desire or inclination, &c.

def: v.n. (prov.) = rījhnā, q.v. v.n. To be rejoiced, be pleased, be delighted (with, -se), to be gratified.
v.t. To ravish, enchant, charm, delight, please; to excite, annoy, vex, tease, plague, perplex.
more: v. a. S. रञ्जन. 1. To please; gladden; enliven. 2. To enchant; captivate; ravish. Sakhī sab pīv saṅg jhūleṅ o gāveṅ, Rasīle rāg se pī ko rijhāveṅ . Song. Bārah-māsa . My friends all with their love-mates swinging sing secure, With warm impassioned strains their husbands
v. a. 1. To please. 2. ( met. ) To plague, to tease, to perplex.

def: v.n. To be rejoiced, be pleased, be delighted (with, -se), to be gratified.
more: v. n. See rijhānā , v. a. To be pleased or merry; to enjoy. 1. Tulsī, apne Rām ko rījh bhaje ke khīj, Khet paṛeṅ sab ūpjeṅ, ulṭe sūdhe bīj . Tulsī Dās. O Tulsī! ever praise your Ram, or pleased or vexed your soul! All seed thrown on the ground doth bear which-ever side it roll. 2. Tum par bālam rījh gaë, tum oṛho na kālī kamalyā ! Song. Your spouse's heart is yours, fair one, though blanket black you should put on! 3. Rījheṅge to īṅeṇ hī māreṅge . Prov. Though pleased but brickbats only thrown. (Evil for good). 4. Rānī gaīṅ hāṭ, lāīṅ rījh-kar chākī ke pāṭ . Prov. A queen went to the mart, a flour mill took her heart. (She had never known before how the corn was ground).
v. n. To be pleased, to be gratified, to be delighted.

def: adj. Having the quality of being pleased.
adj. Having the quality of being pleased; easily pleased or gratified.
adj. = rijhwār, q.v. adj. Having the quality of being pleased; easily pleased or gratified.

ety: rījh(nā), q.v.+wār = wāl or wālā; and ˚waiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: adj. Having the quality of being pleased; easily pleased or gratified.
more: adj. Having the quality of being pleased.

ety: rījh(nā), q.v.+wār = wāl or wālā; and ˚waiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: adj. Having the quality of being pleased; easily pleased or gratified.
more: adj. Having the quality of being pleased.

def: v. n. (dakh.) See ريجهنا rījhnā.
adj. Straight, right; upright, honest, sincere
s.f. = S رجتو ऋजुत्व r̤ijutva, s.m. Straightness; rectitude, honesty.
s.m. = S رجتا ऋजुता r̤ijutā, s.f. Straightness; rectitude, honesty.
s.f. A verse of the Rig-veda; — the Rig-veda
more: s. Name of one of the vedas. See بيد bed.
subs: s.m. The constellation of the Bear
vulg. r̤ig-bed, s.m. The Rig-veda, the first and most important of the four Vedas (so called because it consists chiefly of hymns or sacred verses in praise of the Hindū deities).

def: s.m. pl. (of ruqʻa), Notes, &c.; — (in Pers., as also riqāʻī), sing. A kind of handwriting.
more: s. 1. A kind of handwriting. 2. Plur. of رقعه ruḳʼaﻌ, q. v.

ety: v.n. of رکب 'to ride, mount,' &c.; — used in Persian with amplification of signification
def: s.f. A stirrup; — train, equipage, retinue, cavalcade; — a catch word at the bottom of a page (to indicate the first word of the following page); — a tall and eight-sided goblet, a goblet, bowl; a dish, plate ( = rikābī)
more: n. m. رکب he mounted. A stirrup. Rakāb meṅ pāoṅ haiṅ. rakāb-divāl , n. f. A stirrup-leather. pā ba-rakāb , adv. Ready to start. ham-rakāb , adv. In company with; along with.
(from رکب) s. f. 1. A stirrup. 2. Equipage, train. رکابدار rikāb-dār, A companion, a stirrup-holder. رکاب دوال rikāb-doʼāl, A stirrup-leather.
s. f. A dish, a plate. رکاب دار rikāb-dār, Keeper of the dishes.
subs: s.m. A stirrup-holder, a servant who runs at the stirrup (of a great man), an attendant, a groom; — a cup-bearer; — keeper of the dishes, pantry-man, steward, butler; — a manufacturer and vender of preserves, pickies, and the like
adv. 'With foot in stirrup,' ready to start
adv. In attendance (on, -ke); in company (with), along (with).

def: s.m. pl. (of raqaba), Necks; slaves, servants.
more: s. (plur. of رقبة) Necks, slaves, servants.

def: s.f. A broad flat dish, a plate, a copper saucer; — a drinking cup, a bowl; — a kind of rupee current in Lakhnau
more: n. f. A dish; a plate; a copper saucer. Hamārī pulāo kī rakābī kahīṅ nahīṅ gaī . My plate of pulāo is not gone. (I am safe, whatever happens). The saying is ascribed to Mahommed Shāh who was absorbed in the pleasures of the song and dance when Nādir Ṣhāh appeared at the gates of Delhi. rakābī-mazhab , n. m. A parasite; timeserver.
s. f. 1. A dish, a plate. 2. A kind of rupee current in Lucknow ( Lakhnau ). رکابي مذهب rikāb-ī-maẕʼhab, adj. Parasitical.

def: s.f. A dish, plate (see rikābī).
more: (prop. رکابي) s. f. A dish, a plate.

ety: rt. Zend rić = S. रिच्; cf. S. रेक; and see rekh-ta
def: s.f. Purging; thin excrement in diarrhœa
more: Mah. Wom. n. f. S. रेखा 1. Line ( rekhā , 1). 2. The black lines between the teeth, made by applying missī , q. v. Aëṅ! kuārī beṭī ke dāṅtoṅ meṅ missī kī rekheṅ! A spinster yet, and teeth with jet streaks set! (Only married women are permitted to apply missī ).
s. m. Thin excrement in diarrhœa. ريخ نکلنا rīḵẖ nikalnā, A term of abuse.

def: s.m. (Gaṛhwālī) = rīćh, q.v. s.m. (f. -in, or -nī), A bear
s.m. corr. of r̤ishi, q.v. (cf. also rikhī).
s.m. A sage, saint, &c. (see r̤ishi)
more: n. f. 1. See رشي 2. ( Brokers ) Six rupees. rikhī asīr . ( Brokers ) Sixteen rupees. rikhī mainā . ( Brokers ) Twenty-six rupees.
s. m. A sage. See رکهـ rikh.

def: (comp. of ऋषि A sage, and ईश Lord) s. m. A chief of the r̤ikh̤is or saints.

ety: for A. رقة, inf. n. of رقّ 'to be thin,' &c.
def: s.f. Thinness, attenuated state or condition, attenuation, flimsiness, want of consistency; minuteness, subtileness; — tender-heartedness, mercy, compassion, pity, sympathy; affection; softness; — weeping; — ecstasy, frenzy, religious transport, enthusiasm.
more: n. f. رق to be thin. 1. Thinness; subtileness, attenuation; liquidity. 2. Melting of the heart; pity; tenderness; sympathy; 3. Weeping. 4. See hāl , 7. riqqat-i-manī . Thinness of the seminal discharge.
(from رقّ Heb. To be thin) s. f. 1. Thinness, minuteness, attenuation, subtleness. 2. Affection. 3. Pity, sympathy, tenderness. 4. Weeping.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A bear (vulg. rīćh); — a star; a constellation.
part. adj. & s.m. Emptied, cleared; empty, void, destitute (of); — a vacuity, vacuum; — a wood, forest
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit रक्त (rakta).

def: s.f. Emptiness, vacuity, void. empty space, space.

ety: Zend iri or ri = S. री+Zend aff. man = S. मन्
def: s.f. Pus, matter, humour, rheum; — dregs, dross, lees (of wine).
more: s. f. Pus, matter, humours, dregs, dross, lees, rheum.

def: adj. Mattery, purulent, suppurative
subs: s.f. Piles with a white or pale mucous discharge.

def: n. f. rīm pus, bavāsīr piles. Piles, with white pale matter.
adv. With a pattering sound, patter-patter (as rain).
more: adv. The pattering sound of a light shower. 1. Bhādoṅ bhaë bhārī lage, rim jhim barse meṅh, Ek taraf kī pīṭ kā kaise baṛhe saneh? Bārā-māsā. August too heavy weighs, the rain comes pattering down, My love unshared, unfed, how can it thus be grown? 2. Rim jhim rim jhim mehā barse, yeh pānī kit jāëgā,? Ādhā pānī īṅḍe, mīṅḍe, ādhe se mhārā bhāī nahāëgā . Girls' song. Patter patter rain comes down, where will it all be gone? Half in the ground, and half a shower-bath on my brother thrown.
wety: Onomatopoeic. Compare Bengali রিমঝিম (rimojhim).

def: s.m. An obligation; debt; — (in Alg.) a negative quantity, minus
more: E. n. m. S. ऋण 1. A debt ( qarz ). 1. Haryā, Har se het kar jūṅ kisān kī rīt, Dām ghanerā, rin ghanā, tabhūṅ khet se prīt . O Haryā! give thy love to Har as peasant loves his field, Though rents be high and large his debts, his field he will not yield. 2. Ūṅch baṅṛerī, phokaṛ bāṅs, rīn khāë barhoṅ mās . E. Prov. Ridge pole high and bamboo hollow all through the year have need to borrow. 2. ( Math .) A negative quantity; minus .
s. f. 1. Borrowing, debt. 2. Negative quantity in algebra.
subs: s.m. The clearing off a debt
adj. & s.m. Borrowing; — a borrower, one who contracts debts; — r̤iṇ-grasta, part. adj. Indebted, involved in debt
To take a loan, to borrow
s.m. One who does not pay his debts
s.f. = r̤iṇ-moksh, s.m. Discharge of a debt; freedom from debt.

def: s.m. = rīćh, q.v. s.m. (f. -in, or -nī), A bear
s. m. A bear. See ريچهـ rīchh.

ety: prob. connec. with S. रद् or रन्ध; cf. randa
def: s.m. A sceptic; a knave, rogue; a lewd fellow, reprobate, drunkard, debauchee, blackguard, profligate, libertine, rake (pl. rindān).
more: n. m. 1. A drunkard; reprobate; profligate. 2. A freethinker; a sceptic. 1. Yā to rind-rinde, yā fatah-chande . Prov. As poor as a beggar, or as rich as Crœsus. 2. Sharābeṅ ek sī sab haiṅ, barāṅḍī kyā, tahūrā kyā! Rind. Gazab hai ham to ṭhaire rind, zāhid pārsā ṭhairā! All wine is wine—the drink of saints in Heaven as well as men— Why call me drunkard while the godly man a saint's called then? rind mashrab , A freethinker.
s. m. A reprobate, a drunkard, a debauchee, a rake, a libertine, a profligate, a blackguard. (Pers. plur. رندان).

def: s. (dakh.) Profligacy, debauchery, dissoluteness ( Harris ): see رندي rind-ī.

ety: abst. s. fr. P. rindak, or rinda, fr. rind, q.v.
def: s.f. Dissoluteness, lewdness, profligacy, debauchery, fraud, &c. ( = rindī).
adj. Dissolute, lewd, disorderly, licentious, profligate, rakish, like a debauchee.
more: (from رند) adj. Licentious, rakish, like a debauchee.

def: v.t. To dress (victuals), to cook, to boil.

v. a. To dress (victuals), to cook.

def: s.f. Trick, fraud; lewdness, debauchery.
more: n. f. 1. ( Mah. Wom .) A woman; wench. 2. ( ranḍī munḍī ) A woman of the town; a prostitute; strumpet; whore. Ranḍī fuqīr kar de dam meṅ shah-i-zaman ko; Bad-fan kare palak meṅ insān-i-nek fan ko . Munsīf. A woman makes a beggar of the ruler of his day, The virtuous man she into courses evil doth betray. ranḍī-bāz , n. m. A rake; a gay Lothario; a fornicator; whoremonger. ranḍī-bāzī , n. f. Wenching; fornication; adultery; whoredom.

def: adj. (dakh.) Effeminate, finical, base ( Harris ).
n. f. Fine bluish muslin.

more: v. n. S. रिङ्गण to creep. To creep; crawl.

def: s.f. Envy; emulation, rivalry; equality; comparison
more: n. f.S. ईर्षा or स्पर्ध Emulation ( barābarī , 4). 1. Ris bhalī, havas burī . Prov. To emulate is good, to lust is bad. 2. Chandā niksat jag nive, sabhī nivāveṅ sīs , Tū do-kauṛī kī kāmnī, kyā kare chāṅd kī rīs? Play. The moon uprisen, the world bows down, all heads low lie, A woman but two couries worth, and with the moon to vie! rīs k. , v. a. To vie or compete with; rival.

or safaid rīsh, s.m. Grey-beard, old man.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Offended, displeased, vexed, angry, wrathful, passionate.
adj. Passionate.

(from ريش) adj. Having a long beard.
adj. Having a long beard, long-bearded.

s.m. A singer or author of sacred hymns, an inspired poet or sage, a seer, a bard; a pious person, saint, saint-sage, an anchorite; — a Veda or sacred composition: (the Rishis are seven in number, viz. Kutsa, Atri, Rebha, Agastya, Kuśika, Vasishṭha, and Vyaśva; they constitute a peculiar class of beings in the early mythical system, as distinct from gods, men, Asurs, &c.; — they form, in Astronomy, the constellation of the Great Bear; and the term r̤ishi is also applied to the seventh asterism of the same constellation; the Rishis, in the later sense of 'saints,' or 'anchorites,' are divided into several orders, as Devarshi, Maharshi, Rājarshi, &c.; in Hindī poetry the word r̤ishi is a symbol for seven)
more: n. m. An inspired sage; a saint. sapt-rishī ; H. baṛā rīchh; A. dub-i-asgar. lit . the seven saints. ( Ast .) Ursamajor ; Charles's Wain.
subs: s.f. The wife of a Rishi
s.m. 'Lord of the stars of the Great Bear,' an epithet of the moon
s.m. An obligation owing to the Rishis, i.e. the study of the Vedas
s.f. The daughter of a Rishi; an epithet of Sītā
r̤ishīś, r̤ishīśvar (˚shi+īs˚), s.m. 'Lord or chief of the Rishis,' an epithet of any one of the four sons of Brahmā.

s.m. = S رشتا ऋषिता r̤ishitā, s.f. The order or state of a Rishi, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of rishtan, 'to twist'; rt. Zend rić = S. रिच्; cf. S. रेचितः
def: s.m. Thread, string, line; series; connexion, relationship, kin; relation by blood or marriage; alliance, affinity; the hair-worm or guinea-worm
more: n. m. 1. ( Poet .) A string; line ( ḍorā ). 2. Kin; relation; connection; relation by blood or marriage. 3. Affinity; alliance. Jab tū huā faqīr to nātā kisī se kyā? Chhoṛā kuṭam to phir rahā rishtā kisī se kyā? Matlab bhalā faqīr ko bābā kisī se kyā? Nazīr. The ascetic's holy life embraced bonds you have now with none, Your own relations left, with whom relations should you own? O man of God! naught you've to do with aught this earth upon! rishta-i-haqīqī , n. m. Relation by the full blood.
s. m. 1. A string, thread, line. 2. Series. 3. Relationship, connexion, kin. رشته دار rishta-dār, s. m. A relation, kinsman. رشته داري rishta-dār-ī, s. f. Relationship.
subs: vulg. rishte-dār, s.m. Relation, kinsman; a dependent
vulg. rishte-dārī, s.f. Relationship, alliance, affinity
(-se), To form a connexion or alliance (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رِشْتَه (rišta, “thread”).

n. m. Relation; relative. rishta k. , v. a. To form an alliance or connexion with. rishte-dār-i-baīd ; H. dūr kā nātā , A distant relation. rishte-dār-i-qarīb ; H. pās kā nātā , A near relation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رشته دار (rišta-dār), from رشته (rišta) + دار (dār). Compare Punjabi ਰਿਸ਼ਤੇਦਾਰ (riśtedār).

def: n. f. Relationship; alliance; affinity. rishta-i-sababī, yā nasabī , G. G. Connection by marriage or by consanguinity.
s.f. Spear; lance; sword
subs: adj. & s.m. Armed with a spear; — a spear-bearer.

ety: for A. رشوة, v.n. fr. رشو 'to give a bribe'
def: s.f. A gift for corrupting a judge, a bribe
more: n. f. رشو he bribed him. A bribe; an illegal gratification; hush-money ( mūṅh-bharī ). rishvat-khor ; Pop. rishvat-khorā , n. m. One who takes bribes. rishvat-dihī , n. f. G. G. Giving a bribe. rishvat denā , v. a. To offer or give a bribe; to bribe. rishvat-sitānī , n. f. The act of taking bribes; bribery. rishvat sitānī ba-jabr . Exaction of a bribe. rishvat se milānā . To buy off (a person). rishvat-i-shartī ; H. tirāī , n. f. A contingent bribe. rishvat-i-gair mashrūt , n. An absolute or unconditional bribe (H. ḍubāī , 2.). rishvat khānā, yā lenā , v. n. To swallow or take a bribe; to be bribed. rishvat o bad kirdārī , n. Bribery and corruption.

s. f. A bribe. رشوت خور rishwat-ḵẖor, One who takes bribes. رشوت خوري rishwat-ḵẖor-ī, s. f. The taking of bribes. رشوت دينا rishwat denā, To give a bribe, to bribe; bribery. رشوت کهانا rishwat khānā, To be bribed.
subs: (-ko), To give a bribe (to), to bribe; — s.m. Bribing, bribery
s.f. The act of taking bribes, bribery
rishwat lenā, To swallow or take a bribe, to be bribed
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رِشْوَه (rišwa), from Arabic رِشْوَة (rišwa). First attested in c. 1654 as Middle Hindi رشوت (rśvt).

def: imperf. part. of risnā, q.v.
s.f. General course or way, method, mode, manner, fashion, sort; usage, observance, custom, practice, habit; rite, ceremony; rule, regulation; plan, scheme (syn. rasm; qāʻida; tarkīb)
more: n. f. S. रीति See rasm , Rite; ceremony; observance ( chāl , 4). 1. Rīt, na satvāṅsā, merā 'lāḍlā naṅvāsā !' Prov. Nor seventh month rite nor feast, yet her 'dear grandson' called! 2. Jaisan dekhī gāoṅ kī rīt, taisan karī log se parīt . E. Prov. As is the village custom meet the people so you ought to greet. (Do in Rome as Rome does). 3. Sukh kāran banāyā ek mandar, Paun na jāve vāke andar, Is mandar kī rīt divānī , Bichhāveṅ āg aur oṛheṅ pānī . Riddle. Ans. Hammām . A Turkish bath. A pleasant house one did provide, No air at all could get inside, The practice here you would call mad, On fire they stand, in water clad! 4. Bāt kahan kī rīt māṅ hai antar adhikāë, Ek bachan teṅ ris baḍhe, ek bachan teṅ jāë . Brind satsaī. A mighty power in modes of speech doth dwell, One fans a flame, another it doth quell. rīt dikhānā , v. a. To point out a course of proceeding; to direct. rīt rasm, rīt ra…
s. f. Custom, usage, rite, habit, regulation, rule.
subs: s.f. Manners and customs, usages, observances, &c.
adv. Methodically; in a (certain) manner (e.g. is rīt-se, 'in this way'; 'accordingly'; burī rīt-se, 'in a bad manner,' 'badly'); according to custom
or be-rīt, adj. Contrary to custom, irregular, unlawful; ill-fashioned; disagreeable; bad
To introduce a new custom, &c.; to make an innovation.

( Butchers ) n. m. Fat ( charbī ).

ety: prop. رئا riʼā, inf. n. of iii of رأي 'to see'
def: s.m. Acting ostentatiously; affectation, show, pretence, hypocrisy, dissimulation, subterfuge, evasion
more: n. m. Hypocrisy. See du-rangī. riyā k . To dissemble; play the hypocrite. riyā-kār , adj. See du-bhāo. riyā-kār , n. m. A dissembler; hypocrite.
(from رأي To see) s. m. Hypocrisy, dissimulation, subterfuge, evasion, affectation. ريا کار riyā-kār, Hypocrite, dissembler.
subs: adj. & s.m. Hypocritical, deceitful, double-faced; — hypocrite, dissembler
To practise hypocrisy, to dissemble, to act ostentatiously.

def: s.m. The lungs, the lights
more: s. m. (See plur. ريات) The lungs. ذات الريه ẕātu-r-riya, s. m. An inflammation of the lungs, the peripneumony.
subs: Inflammation of the lungs (see s.v. zāt).

def: s.f. pl. (of rīḥ), Winds, airs; flatus, flatulence.
more: n. f. Wind in the stomach. Riyāh rukne se peṭ meṅ dard ho jātā hai .

def: (from ريا q. v.) s. m. f. A hypocrite, a sophist.
s.m. Hypocrite ( = riyā-kār); sophist.

ety: for A. رئاسة, inf. n. of رأس 'to be or become high in rank'
def: s.f. Governing, ruling; government, dominion, sway, rule, headship, command; nobility, nobleness, highmindedness
more: n. f. راس became chief. 1. Nobility; nobleness; high-mindedness. 2. See rāj , 2, 3. 1. Inke mizāj meṅ baṛī riyāsat hai . 2. Riyāsat be-syāsat nahīṅ . Prov. For king and rule rod and ferule. riyāsat-i-jamhūrī , n. f. A republic; democracy. riyāsat se khārij . G. G. An outlaw.
(from رأس) s. f. Government, command, dominion, sway.
ex: بادشاہانِ دہلی کے تصرف میں ملتان آگیا ، جدا ریاست نہ رہی۔
Multan came under the control of the Delhi rulers, it was no longer a separate state.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ریاست (riyāsat), from Arabic رِئَاسَة (riʔāsa).

def: s.m. pl. (of ريه riya, q.v.), Lungs, lights.
more: (plur. of رٴية from رأي) s. f. The lungs.

def: s.f. = riyāẓat, q.v. s.f. pl. (of riyāẓat), Exercises, religious exercises, devotions, austerities.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic رياضة f (riyāḍa).

def: s.m. pl. (of rauẓa, q.v.), Gardens.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic رياضة f (riyāḍa).

ety: for A. رياضة, inf. n. of روض 'to train, to discipline'
def: s.f. Training, discipline; exercise; labour, toil; religious exercise, devotion, abstinence, abstemiousness, mortifying the flesh, austerity
more: n. f. روض subdued himself. 1. Labour; toil. 2. Austerity ( tap ). ryāz k., riyāz kar-ke khānā , v. a. 1. To earn by one's labour; to work hard. 2. To live honestly.
(from راض Heb. To exercise, to tame, to run) s. f. Abstinence, austerity, abstemiousness. (Plur. رياضات).
subs: (or riyāẓ) karnā, To exercise oneself; to labour; to discipline; to practise abstinence, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ریاضت f (riyāzat), from Arabic رِيَاضَة f (riyāḍa).

def: s.f. pl. (of riyāẓat), Exercises, religious exercises, devotions, austerities.
s.m. One who exercises himself, an active or industrious person; — a devotee, an ascetic, one who practises austerities.
more: n. m. 1. An industrious person. 2. One who practises austerities; an ascetic; a devotee.
s. m. A practiser of austerities, a devotee.

def: s.m. A mathematician.
more: n. m. روض trained. Mathematics. riyāzī-dāṅ , n. m. A mathematician.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ریاضی (riyāzī), from Arabic رِيَاضِيّ (riyāḍiyy).

def: adj. corr. of رذال riẕāl, q.v.
more: (for رذال ruẕāl, from رذل) adj. Mean, worthless.

def: adj. & s.m. corr. but com. form of riẕāla, q.v.
more: (for رذاله ruẕāla ) adj. Worst, after the best has been picked out; mean, vile, base.

ety: for A. رذالة ruẕālat, fr. ruẕāl, v.n. fr. رذل 'to be low, or base,' &c.
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Low, mean, base, bad, vile, contemptible; — the worse or viler (or the worst or vilest), the refuse, or dregs (of anything), a thing of which the good has been picked out; a mean, low, or vile person; an impudent or shameless fellow.
more: adj. rizāl'ā, razīl , n. m. A vagabond; villain; an impudent or shameless person. 1. Razīl kī do, na ashrāf kī sau . Prov. The blackguard's two words to the respectable man's hundred. (Foul language). 2. Bhalā mānas ghar meṅ baṛā, rizāle ne jānā mujh seḍarā . Prov. Respectability home shies off clear, the street rough says 'he is afraid to meet me here.' rizālī bāt , Obscene language ( baknī bāteṅ ). rizāl-pan, rizāl-panā , n. m. Meanness; baseness; worthlessness.

ety: riẕāl+Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), and त्व+कं
def: s.m. Low estate, low or mean condition or circumstances; lowness, meanness, baseness, vileness, worthlessness, badness, corruptness.
s. m. Meanness, baseness.

ety: riẕāl+Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), and त्व+कं
def: s.m. Low estate, low or mean condition or circumstances; lowness, meanness, baseness, vileness, worthlessness, badness, corruptness.

ety: inf. n. of رزق; see razzāq
def: s.m. Means of subsistence or support, subsistence, food, daily bread; subsistence money, allowance, pension
more: n. m. Daily bread; subsistence money; an allowance (اجيوکا). 1. Mere sāmne rizq hai . An oath. I swear on the food before me. 2. Rizq hai na maut! Prov. Nor food will come to me nor death! 3. Kam-rizqe bahut hain, be-rizqā koī nahīṅ . Prov. Many under-fed there are, without food no one! 4. Palle rizaq nahīṅ bāṅdhte panchhī aur darvesh, Jin ko takyah Rab kā unko rizaq hamesh . Dohā The birds of air and devotees no food about them keep, Who put their trust in God for lack of food have ne'er to weep. rizq pahuṅchānā yā denā , v. a. To supply food; to support or maintain one.
s. m. Food, wealth, a pension, allowance, support, subsistence, daily bread.
subs: rizq-denā (-ko), To supply food (to), to support, maintain, provide (for).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رِزْق (rizq), from Arabic رِزْق (rizq), from Middle Persian 𐭫𐭥𐭰𐭩𐭪 (rōzīg). Doublet of روزِی (rōzī).
